decisions.reasoning: Flare Blitz is a guaranteed OHKO on Flutter Mane, removing a high-speed threat immediately. Since I can secure the KO, I should prioritize taking the opponent's Pokemon off the field rather than switching.

Incineroar is at 51% and will be OHKOed by Close Combat or 2HKOed by Surging Strikes. Amoonguss resists both of Urshifu-Rapid's STAB types, making it the safest pivot to neutralize the immediate threat.

Urshifu-Rapid Strike is a major threat, and Spore is the best way to neutralize it immediately. Since Amoonguss survives Close Combat, taking the risk to put the opponent to sleep provides the best momentum shift.
Urshifu-Rapid is already asleep, and since Amoonguss is slower, Spore is the best way to maintain pressure or catch a switch-in if they decide to move Urshifu out.

Urshifu-Rapid-Strike is already asleep (as noted in the HP status), so Spore is a wasted turn. However, since the opponent is likely to switch out their sleeping Urshifu, using Spore again is a safe prediction to catch a switch-in or force them to deal with the sleep pressure if they stay in.
The opponent's Urshifu-Rapid-Strike is already asleep (as indicated in the HP status), so I will capitalize on the free turn to maintain pressure or force a switch while they are incapacitated.
Urshifu-Rapid is already asleep (SLP status noted in HP status), so using Spore again is a safe play to keep it incapacitated while I maintain positioning, or it forces a switch from the opponent.

Urshifu-Rapid is already asleep, so clicking Spore is a safe way to maintain pressure or catch a switch-in if they decide to pivot out. Since I cannot KO and the opponent is forced to either stay in and waste turns or switch, Spore is the highest value play.

Amoonguss is threatened with an OHKO by Flare Blitz and cannot effectively damage Incineroar. Switching to my own Incineroar provides a Fire-type resistance to absorb the Flare Blitz and allows me to pivot or intimidate the opponent.
The opponent's Incineroar is asleep, making this a free opportunity to pivot out and gain momentum while lowering their stats, setting up a better matchup for my bench.
The opponent's Incineroar is asleep, making it a sitting duck. Knock Off is the best play to remove its item while dealing chip damage without risking recoil from Flare Blitz.
